projects.name: Colombo
projects.overview: Optimizing store layout based on category efficiency trying to maximize store sales
my_part: codeveloping the project throught the whole project cycle, starting from business requirement understanding to deploying to production
analysing large data sets to find right solutions for problems, or discovering hidden features in the data. The analysis was performed using Pyspark, Plotly and Databricks
analysis and deep dive into model effects to understand the predictive power of the model, and its ability to predict category efficeincies
created processing pipeline starting from raw tables up to the final business output. The processing was productionalized using Azure Data Factiry (ADF)
For the great part I was responsible for handling all the furniture and total store capacity requirements, so I had to communicate with project stakeholders
Integrated Azure Machine Learning into optimization process, improving the computation time by 20% and allowing the optimization to be performed larger store groups
projects.project_impact: The project is estimated to improve yearly sales in small stores by 7%
projects.tools_used: python, pyspark, pandas, plotly, pytest, Azure, Azure Data Factory, Azure Machine Learning, Databricks, Docker, git

projects.name: Midas
projects.overview: Optimizing store book assortment to maximze book store sales and reduce restocking
my_part: Performed store segmentation using sklearn clustering and XGBoost classifier which improved supply chain operations by reducing unique store-category layouts
Build pipeline to extract segmentation results into external tools used by the company
projects.project_impact: The project is in the production stage for multiple years and it significantly improved stores book assortment and reduced supply chain operations
projects.tools_used: python, pyspark, pandas, plotly, pytest, scikit-learn, xgboost, Azure, Azure Data Factory, Azure Machine Learning, Databricks, Docker, git

projects.name: Pricing
projects.overview: Analysis around pricing and price elasticity as well as dynamic pricing
my_part: Developed basis for a Dynamic-Pricing-Model model, and analysed results using SQL and GCP BigQuery. Also suggested products that tested the pricing model using stratified sampling and product features
Conducted price and elasticity analysis among customers reducing unnecessary promotions and optimizing pricing policy
projects.project_impact: The project increased monthly revenue from the test sample by 8%.
projects.tools_used: python, scikit-learn, SQL, BigQuery, Google Cloud Platform

projects.name: Product offer
projects.overview: Managing and adding new products to the offer
my_part: Analyzed and added products to the portfolio, contributing to a 20% increase in assortment. Additionally collaborated on pricing and purchasing strategy planning, improving product utilization by 4%.
Analyzed the offer to find new complementary products or services
projects.project_impact: Contribution to 20% increase in assortment and unique products offered. Improved product utilization by 4% which was achieved by buying and selecting more efficient products.
projects.tools_used: SQL, BigQuery, Google Cloud Platform, Excel, PowerQuery

projects.name: Other
projects.overview: Other tasks, resolved around work automation
my_part: Developed multiple automation tools using python and selenium which decreased the manual work on the offer.
Supervised the operation of a Scrapy scraper collecting competitor prices, enhancing adjustments based on competitor actions.
projects.project_impact: Manual work reduction time, and optimized data gathering process
projects.tools_used: python, selenium, scrapy, SQL, BigQuery, Google Cloud Platform

personal_projects.name: Technical specification extraction tool
personal_projects.overview: a tool to extract technical specification metrics of solar panels from pdf. It used OCR method to extract text from PDF, and then local Ollama model with langchain and pydantic to extract only the required specifications.
personal_projects.tools: Langchain, pydantic, ollama, python, Docker, uv

personal_projects.name: Paint by number kit generator
personal_projects.overview: a tool to create paint by number kits from input image. It turned images into painting imitations using sklearn Kmeans clustering, then processed them using flooding algorithm, and then created numbered outlines.
personal_projects.tools: Scikit-learn, OpenCV, numpy, gradio, python, uv

personal_projects.name: Uplift prediction model
personal_projects.overview: The goal of this project is to enhance the targeting efficiency of the marketing campaign aimed at persuading customers to subscribe to the term deposit product. It used scikit-learn tools and models, polars and seaborn for EDA and data manipulation. The modeling used xgboost and sklift package and appropriete evaluation metrics.
personal_projects.tools: Scikit-learn, XGBoost, polars, pandas, seaborn, matplotlib

personal_projects.name: Choice experiment survey
personal_projects.overview: Created a webapp with custom survey engine which allowed to gather choice preferences and calculate willingness-to-pay. Utilized FastAPI backend with supabase, and React on the frontend. The survey was deployed using GCP run, and choice modelling was performed with R mlogit package
personal_projects.tools: FastAPI, React, python, typescript, R, Docker, supabase

personal_projects.name: GPT-2 training in R
personal_projects.overview:
This project aims to reimplement the GPT-2 model 124M parameters model in R and recreates the zero-shot evaluation of GPT-2 from "Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners" (Radford et al., 2019) on three key benchmarks:
    1. Language Modeling: Perplexity scores on Penn Treebank (PTB) and WikiText-2 to measure generative performance.
    2. Reading Comprehension: Zero-shot F1 score on the CoQA dataset, where the model answers questions conditioned on a document and conversation history.

personal_projects.name: Auchan products scraper
personal_projects.overview: This project implemented an automated Scrapy tool to extract  product information from Auchan website. It utilized additional scrapy middlewares to rotate headers, and used session cookies for authentications. Also it used selenium for automatic popup adds denial

personal_projects.name: Syntetic orders data generator
personal_projects.overview: This module helps generate synthetic orders based on assumed distributions and factors. Each order is distributed across Periods with calculated probabilties. Then those probabilities are used to generate DataFrame of orders with given products.

achievements.name: HackYeah 2024 Hackathon
achievements.overview: Built a tool which extract text, sound and motion features from short public speech videos, and grade the speech. It used OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text, DeepFace for motion features extraction and librosa for audio analysis. We did not get any prize but managed to create a working solution in 24 hours.
achievements.tools: Python, Docker, OpenAI whisper, numpy, nltk, OpenAI API
